descriptions.text:
A survival platformer for your NES or favorite emulator.

"I swear it happened this way officer!"
It was supposed to be your day off. But you went in to fix a "glitch" at the Rebuild Life robot factory. What could possibly go wrong?

Hazard: Let Us Out! A survival platformer.

"I swear it happened this way officer!"

It was supposed to be your day off. But you went in to fix a "glitch" at the Rebuild Life robot factory. What could possibly go wrong?

Do you have what it takes? Battle your way past crazy bosses, unlock multiple weapons, solve puzzles and make the right choices to save the day! As you fight your way through 200+ screens of robotic rampage & mechanized mayhem you will have to make decisions, and your choices certainly matter, especially to the scientists trapped in the Rebuild Life Robotics Factory. Depending on how you played through the game, you will receive one of multiple endings. This game was designed to be fun not frustrating. Hazard is generous with re-spawn points and gives you the choice to skip the more challenging bosses. But be warned, your choices matter!

Did you do it? Did you save the scientists? Did you just trigger a robopocolypse? Only one way to find out. Grab a copy of Hazard for your NES and get to work!

Hazard: Let us out was created by Gilbert Vera for play on the NES.
